intro: Converting from Java to Go strips out the heavy runtime abstraction of the JVM in favor of a lean, compiled language that outputs static binaries. This migration is typically executed to minimize container startup latency, slash memory consumption, and optimize microservice architecture scaling profiles. The translation involves discarding object-oriented inheritance frameworks for Go's simplified composition-over-inheritance design.

diffs.from: Statically typed, nominal object-oriented design emphasizing deep inheritance hierarchies and class-based polymorphism.
diffs.to: Statically typed, structural layout favoring implicit interfaces and composition over inheritance paths.

diffs.from: Highly verbose, enterprise-oriented design requiring boilerplate classes, packages, and public/private declarations per file.
diffs.to: Minimalist and strict, optimizing for code readability with reduced keywords and capital-letter-based visibility tracking.

diffs.from: Heavy virtual machine (JVM) heap optimization using complex generational garbage collection schemes.
diffs.to: Direct compilation to machine code with a low-latency, concurrent garbage collector optimizing for stack allocation.

diffs.from: Thread pools (ExecutorService) or Virtual Threads managed by the runtime framework over OS-level scheduling.
diffs.to: Native Communicating Sequential Processes (CSP) via lightweight, runtime-scheduled goroutines and native channels.

diffs.from: Large enterprise frameworks bundled with a deep, historical hierarchy for reflection, manipulation, and UI components.
diffs.to: Pragmatic, modern toolset designed natively for web systems, networking protocols, tooling, and low-level parsing.

examples.title: Class Implementation vs Struct Composition
examples.description: Converting an inheritance/interface class to a Go struct with implicit interface alignment.

examples.title: Concurrent Execution
examples.description: Replacing thread pool executors with modern, native goroutines.

tips.title: Flatten Class Trees
tips.body: Do not attempt to replicate extended class lineages. Unroll them completely using embedded Go structs to share data layouts instead.

tips.title: Reconstruct Exception Flow
tips.body: Eliminate try-catch structures. Explicitly evaluate and return instances of the 'error' interface as the final argument from Go functions.

tips.title: Avoid Package Proliferation
tips.body: Java developers tend to make deep nested directories. Go favors flat, unified package layout structures per functional boundary.

tips.title: Check Pointer Mutability
tips.body: Decide deliberately whether functions need a value receiver (copies data) or a pointer receiver (mutates original struct reference).

faqs.question: Does Go have generics like Java?
faqs.answer: Go introduces type parameters since version 1.18, allowing generic functions and types, but it omits advanced type constraints like variance or wildcards.

faqs.question: How do dependency injections translate?
faqs.answer: Avoid heavy reflection frameworks like Spring. In Go, dependencies are explicitly initialized in your main entrypoint and passed downstream via constructors.

faqs.question: Where is the standard null pointer exception handled?
faqs.answer: Go uses nil. Calling methods on nil pointer receivers will panic, requiring defensive checking before usage.
